Verify the correct account name and password. If you know the unlock code, restart the device and sign in with local administrator credentials. If the device is locked remotely, contact the Service Desk immediately so a manager can review the incident and investigate the lock reason. Do not install unknown remote unlocking tools.

Do not open attachments or click on unknown links in email or social media messages. Use the approved antivirus client and scan all removable media. If prompted, enter the business version of your Microsoft account and device name. If you cannot connect immediately, reboot and test one permitted approved network share. Do not disable MFA unless instructed.

Verify correct account and device name, test another approved network, and restart the app. Repeated failed attempts or large-scale failures require escalated support. Never use unknown open networks. Include the exact error code, time, location, and whether other users are affected.

Immediately notify the Service Desk so security can assess the asset tag, location, time of theft, and last known password. Do not delay reporting or use unofficial recovery tools. If confidential data was exposed, contact Security Operations immediately.

Install approved packages from the package manager or a known-good ISO. If not available, submit a service request with the required asset tag, location, exact version needed, and reason for installation. Do not use unofficial cracks, serials, or unofficial installers.

Verify the correct device type, business network, and location. Reconnect using the approved corporate app and factor in allowed time based on business need. If unsuccessful, contact the Service Desk with asset tag, device type, location, and attempt history. Do not disable MFA or use personal data.

Call the Service Desk immediately so a security incident response team can assess and contain the exposure. Provide the asset tag, device type, last known good time, and whether unknown users were present. MFA must be disabled only as a last resort.

Do not accept unknown USB drives, email attachments, or click on suspicious links. Report the attachment or file type, location, and sender so security can investigate. Use approved antivirus and blocklisted URLs, and test received content in a quarantine folder first. Do not open unofficial update packages or install unknown removable media.

Check battery status and connect to a known-good power source if available. Restart the device, close unnecessary applications, close remote-desktop connections, and check disk space. Reboot the domain controller if possible. Do not disable endpoint protection unless instructed.

Do not share credentials, approve unknown MFA prompts, or click suspicious links. Call the Service Desk with the account name, business reason, time, and location. The approver must verify the request and approve it before the action is allowed. MFA should never be disabled without manager approval.
